objections.description: "React 18 functional components with hooks, loaded as production ES modules from CDN/import map, with no runtime JSX compiler and no bundler requirement" is architecturally contradictory. React without JSX means either htm tagged templates or raw createElement calls across every component. htm adds a runtime template parser (perf tax on every render), and raw createElement is unmaintainable at the component count described (shell, tabbar, bottom-sheet, skeleton, status-card, timeline-item, search-composer, empty-state, 3 screens, state, icons). The spec must pick ONE concrete approach and defend it: (a) htm + preact (smaller, actually designed for no-build), (b) a single-pass esbuild/vite build step that outputs static ES modules (still no dev server required, just a build command), or (c) raw createElement with a thin h() helper. My recommendation: option (b) with esbuild --bundle --splitting --format=esm producing static outputs into src/frontend/web/dist/. This keeps the no-runtime-compiler guarantee, gives you JSX, tree-shakes dead code, and the output is still static files served by web_server.py unchanged.
objections.suggested_fix: Add a minimal build step: `esbuild src/frontend/web/src/main.jsx --bundle --splitting --format=esm --outdir=src/frontend/web/dist --minify`. Source lives in src/frontend/web/src/, built output in src/frontend/web/dist/. index.html references dist/main.js. No dev server, no HMR, no webpack. One command, deterministic output, static serving preserved.

objections.description: "dark-first CSS variables" is stated but no actual token map is provided. This is a spec that must be approved before coding — shipping without a concrete token table means each reviewer is approving a different mental model. We need the actual variable names, their dark values, their light overrides, the type scale steps, spacing scale, and motion curve definitions. Without this, the visual design critic (Qwen) and the frontend engineer (ZAI) cannot meaningfully approve.
objections.suggested_fix: Include a concrete tokens.css draft with at minimum: --surface-{0,1,2,3}, --text-{primary,secondary,tertiary,inverse}, --accent-{default,hover,active}, --status-{success,warning,error,info}, --radius-{sm,md,lg,full}, --shadow-{sm,md,lg}, --space-{1..12} mapped to a 4px base, --font-{xs,sm,base,lg,xl,2xl} with line-heights, --motion-{spring,ease-out-expo,duration-fast,duration-normal}, --z-{sheet,overlay,sticky,dropdown}. Provide both :root (dark) and [data-theme='light'] override blocks.

objections.description: The spec describes screens narratively but provides zero concrete component hierarchy, props contracts, or data flow. 'Primary KPI rail' — what data? From which endpoint? 'Chronological transaction-style timeline' — what's the data shape? What's the API contract? Without a component tree showing parent→child relationships, prop interfaces, and which backend endpoints feed which screens, this is a mood board, not an implementation spec. Four reviewers cannot converge on vibes.
objections.suggested_fix: Add a component architecture map as a concrete tree: App → ThemeProvider → Shell → [TabBar, Screen]. Each Screen lists its child components, their props (with TypeScript-style annotations even if not using TS), and the API endpoint + response shape they consume. Example: HomeScreen → [Header({title:string}), KPIRail({metrics:Metric[]}), StatusCard({status:Status, lastUpdated:Date}), SearchPresets({presets:Preset[]})]. Include api.js function signatures: fetchMetrics():Promise<Metric[]>, fetchTimeline(id:string):Promise<TimelineEntry[]>, etc.

objections.description: Swipe affordance, long-press, and pull-to-refresh on a no-build vanilla JS React app with no gesture library is a significant implementation commitment. The spec doesn't address which gesture library (if any) or whether these are custom pointer event handlers. Custom touch gesture code is a bug factory — especially swipe-to-dismiss with momentum and pull-to-refresh with overscroll containment. This needs scoping: which gestures ship in v1 vs. which are deferred.
objections.suggested_fix: Tier the interaction patterns. v1: tap, keyboard, focus management, bottom sheet drag handle only. v2: swipe affordance on list items, pull-to-refresh. Drop long-press entirely — it has zero discoverability and conflicts with native context menus. If swipe ships in v1, specify @use-gesture/react or a concrete custom implementation approach with touch-action CSS values.

objections.description: Migration path says 'retain legacy artifacts until parity check passes' but doesn't define parity. What's the checklist? Which legacy features must work before the old index.html is removed? Without this, you'll have two frontends in the repo indefinitely — I've seen this movie, it ends with both rotting.
objections.suggested_fix: Define explicit parity checklist: (1) all backend endpoints consumed by old UI are consumed by new UI, (2) all user-facing actions reproducible, (3) lighthouse performance score >= old UI, (4) manual smoke test on iOS Safari, Chrome Android, Chrome Desktop, Firefox Desktop. Set a deadline: legacy files quarantined to src/frontend/web/_legacy/ within 2 weeks of new shell merge, deleted within 4 weeks.

objections.description: Motion system lists class names (spring-in, float, breathe, sway, sheet-lift, badge-pulse) but provides no actual keyframe definitions, duration values, or easing curves. 'Spring-in' is not a CSS-native concept — CSS doesn't have spring physics. The spec must clarify whether this is a cubic-bezier approximation of spring (and provide the exact values) or requires the Web Animations API with a spring solver.
objections.suggested_fix: Define each animation concretely. Example: spring-in: cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) 300ms on transform:scale(0.9)->scale(1) + opacity:0->1. sheet-lift: cubic-bezier(0.32, 0.72, 0, 1) 350ms on transform:translateY(100%)->translateY(0). breathe: @keyframes with scale(1)->scale(1.02)->scale(1) over 3s infinite. All durations in CSS custom properties (--motion-duration-fast: 150ms, --motion-duration-normal: 300ms, etc.).

objections.description: File layout mixes concerns: icons.js as a flat module will grow unbounded. Every SVG icon as a JS export in one file becomes a monolith that defeats any code-splitting benefit mentioned in section 11.
objections.suggested_fix: Split icons into src/frontend/web/app/icons/{home,timeline,search,actions,chevron,status-dot,...}.js — one default export per icon. Or if using esbuild (per objection 1), tree-shaking handles this automatically from a barrel file.

suggestions.description: Home screen says 'skeleton placeholders' but doesn't specify skeleton shape fidelity. High-fidelity skeletons (matching actual content dimensions) dramatically reduce perceived layout shift vs. generic gray bars. Specify that skeleton components mirror the exact height/width/border-radius of their resolved content counterparts.

Accessibility section doesn't mention error announcement strategy. Form validation errors in the action sheet must be announced via aria-live='assertive' or role='alert'. Status updates (section 10 mentions aria-live) should use aria-live='polite' for non-blocking updates. Be explicit about which regions use which politeness level.

No mention of error states for API failures. Every screen needs a defined error state: what does Home look like when fetchMetrics() 500s? What does Timeline show when the endpoint is unreachable? Skeleton-only loading is great, but 'skeleton forever' is worse than a spinner. Define timeout-to-error-state thresholds (e.g., 8s timeout → error state with retry action).

No state management approach specified. state.js is listed but its pattern is undefined. For this app scale, I'd recommend React context + useReducer for global state (theme, auth, active tab) and colocated useState for component-local state. Explicitly ban prop drilling past 2 levels. If data fetching needs caching/deduplication, specify whether you're rolling a minimal fetch cache or using something like SWR (adds a dependency but solves stale-while-revalidate correctly).
